warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: This standard specifies the varieties, scope of use, and maximum usage levels of food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.parameter: Contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex Alimentarius Commission establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants to be legally permitted in food or feed commodities to protect human health. Specific MLs are detailed in standards such as CXS 193-1995.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: A Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.limit: Verification required from Union list in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008
rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food additives must be approved for use in the EU and comply with their conditions of use.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.limit: Verification required from Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food products placed on the market must comply with food safety criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites... These criteria define the acceptability of a product or a batch of food applicable to products placed on the market.

rules.limit: Compliance with food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005, requiring shelf-life studies and sampling procedures
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Mandatory procedures (shelf-life studies, sampling) for food business operators manufacturing RTE food to manage L. monocytogenes risk.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Technical guidance document on shelf-life studies for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods provides specialised laboratories with detailed and practical information on how to conduct shelf-life studies... in order to comply with the food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

rules.limit: Verification required from Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food production processes must comply with process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: In addition, this Regulation lays down certain process hygiene criteria to indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

rules.foodCategory: Certain animal products (e.g., poultry, meat, eggs)
rules.limit: Verification required from Council Decision 95/410/EC, Decision 2004/235/EC, Decision 2003/644/EC, Regulation (EC) No 1688/2005, Regulation (EC) No 853/2004, and Regulation (EC) No 2160/2003
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Special guarantees apply to trade from Sweden, Finland, Norway, and other EU countries/regions with equivalent control programmes for certain animal products.
rules.evidenceQuote: When Sweden and Finland joined the EU special guarantees on Salmonella were provided as regards trade from other countries of certain live animals and products... The current guarantees are laid down in: Council Decision 95/410/EC... Decision 2004/235/EC... Decision 2003/644/EC... Regulation (EC) No 1688/2005... Other EU countries or region of EU countries can obtain special guarantees if it has a control programme recognised as equivalent to that approved for Sweden and Finland in accordance with Regulations (EC) No 853/2004 (foodstuffs) and 2160/2003 (animals).

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of approved food additives and their limits is required.

rules.notes: Verification of regulations concerning BSE in food is required.

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Verification of specific specifications and standards for food contaminants is required.

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of specific limits for food contaminated by radioactivity is required.

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Verification of general food labelling requirements under the Specifications and Standards is required.

rules.parameter: Health Claims, Special Dietary Uses, Nutrition Labeling

rules.notes: Verification of specific requirements for health claims, food for special dietary uses, and nutrition labeling is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food with Health Claims, Food for Special Dietary Uses, and Nutrition Labeling

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of regulations for food produced by recombinant DNA techniques, including potential labelling requirements, is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Produced by Recombinant DNA Techniques

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Verification of specific specifications and standards for microbiological limits is required.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Must have technological justification and not present health risks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Food additives are allowed when there is a technological justification and they do not present health risks to consumers.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives are allowed when: there is a technological justification they do not present health risks to consumers

rules.limit: Do not import or sell, unless SFA approval
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold, unless an application to SFA for evaluation is submitted.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type. Use the List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to check.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA's regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food products containing contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.parameter: Microbiological standards, heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Various limits are imposed on different types of contaminants. Refer to relevant SFA documents for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards [...] Heavy metals [...] Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins [...] Veterinary drug residues [...] Pesticide residues [...] Other incidental constituents.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, which must be complied with.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol [...] hexoestrol [...] dienoestrol

rules.foodCategory: prepacked foods, seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be declared in list of ingredients
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: All ingredients and additives used in prepacked foods for sale in Singapore must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) must be verified in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.limit: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These allergens must be declared if present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Sulphite must be declared if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food

rules.limit: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These allergens are recommended for declaration if present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific pesticide residue limits must be verified in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products must be verified in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.limit: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.limit: Verification required against official regulations and permitted lists.
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Regulations for food additives are established by the Thai FDA and must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.limit: Verification required against official lists of permitted and non-permitted ingredients.
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Official lists of permitted and non-permitted ingredients in food must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: ส่วนประกอบที่อนุญาต/ไม่อนุญาตให้ใช้ในอาหาร

rules.parameter: Contaminants, Toxins, and Veterinary Drug Residues
rules.limit: Verification required against official food standards.
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Food standards for contaminants, toxins, and veterinary drug residues are established by the Thai FDA and must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน, มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.limit: Verification required against the ASEAN MRLs list.
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: The ASEAN MRLs list (Revised February 2569 B.E.) must be consulted for maximum residue limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: บัญชีค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs) (ฉบับปรับปรุง กุมภาพันธ์ 2569)

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Regulations for food labeling and nutrition labeling are established by the Thai FDA and must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.limit: Foods listed as prohibited must not be produced, imported, or sold.
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale exists and must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.limit: Verification required against official food standards.
rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Food standards for pathogenic microorganisms are established by the Thai FDA and must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Manufacturers must verify the regulatory status and approved uses of food and color additives by consulting FDA regulations (CFR), GRAS Notice Inventory, and Substances Added to Food Inventory.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review

rules.parameter: Food additives, color additives, GRAS substances, prior-sanctioned substances
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes food additives (21 CFR Parts 172, 173), color additives (Parts 73, 74), GRAS substances (21 CFR Parts 182, 184), and prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food are labeled 'PROHIBITED' or 'PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a guidance level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.foodCategory: Foods, Peanuts and peanut products, Brazil nuts, Pistachio nuts
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an action level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA's Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and CFR
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Contaminant levels include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry.

rules.parameter: Allergen cross-contact prevention and advisory statements
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Manufacturers must implement allergen preventive controls as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) to prevent cross-contact and undeclared allergens. Advisory statements ('may contain [allergen]') are not required but can be used if good manufacturing practices are followed and cross-contact is unavoidable.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.parameter: Declaration of Major Food Allergens (contains statement)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The allergen's food source must be declared immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.
rules.evidenceQuote: Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement. Example: “Contains wheat, milk, and soy.”

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the name of the ingredient.
rules.evidenceQuote: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. Examples: “lecithin (soy),” “flour (wheat),” and “whey (milk)”

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: A final rule defines 'gluten-free' for food labeling. Compliance requirements also exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling

rules.parameter: Specificity in Major Food Allergen Labeling
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The type of tree nut (e.g., almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (e.g., bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (e.g., crab, lobster, shrimp) must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
